70	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — algebraic generic freeness (finite case)
71	
72	This section builds the **module-theoretic** generic-freeness statements that
73	underlie the geometric `genericFlatness` (blueprint
74	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness").
75	
76	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
77	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
78	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
79	clearing denominators). Mathlib already supplies most of the dévissage
80	machinery (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`,
81	`ModuleCat.free_shortExact`, `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`), but it does
82	**not** yet contain the polynomial-ring core (generic freeness for a finite
83	module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]`). See the file `task_results` handoff for the
84	precise decomposition of the remaining gap.
85	
86	What we *can* land axiom-clean here is the **finite-module / finite-morphism
87	case**: when `M` is finite as an `A`-module (in particular when `B` is
88	module-finite over `A`), generic freeness follows directly from
89	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` applied at the
90	generic point `Frac A`, where `M ⊗_A Frac A` is a finite vector space hence
91	free. This is a genuine special case of the algebraic generic-freeness
92	theorem (the case of a *finite* morphism `X → S`), and a reusable building
93	block for the dévissage's leaves. -/
94	
95	namespace GenericFreeness
96	
97	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
98	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
99	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.
100	
101	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
102	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
103	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
104	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
105	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
106	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
107	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
108	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
109	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
110	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
111	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
112	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
113	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
114	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
115	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩
116	
117	/-- **Generic flatness, finite-module case.** The flatness form of
118	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
119	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
120	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
121	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
122	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
123	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
124	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
125	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
126	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
127	  haveI := hfree
128	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩
129	
130	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
131	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
132	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
133	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
134	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
135	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
136	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
137	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
138	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
139	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
140	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
141	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
142	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
143	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
144	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
145	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
146	
147	/-! ### Dévissage chain for the finite-type residue (Nitsure §4)
148	
149	The following sub-lemmas decompose the surviving residue of
150	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` — the case where `M` is finite over the finite-type
151	`A`-algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A` — into the classical Nitsure §4
152	induction. They are ordered so each step uses only earlier blocks (or Mathlib
153	anchors). Throughout, `A` is a noetherian domain with fraction field
154	`K = Frac A`, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module with
155	the compatible `A`-module structure via the scalar tower `A → B → M`. -/
156	
157	/-- **L1 — torsion base case `M_K = 0`** (`lem:gf_torsion_base`, Nitsure §4 base
158	case of the induction). If the generic localisation `M_K = K ⊗_A M` (encoded as
159	`LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M`) is trivial and `M` is a finite
160	`B`-module, then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `f • M = 0`; consequently
161	`M_f = 0` is free over `A_f`.
162	
163	The hypothesis `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)` is the
164	formal encoding of `M_K = 0`: localising `M` at the non-zero divisors of the
165	domain `A` inverts exactly the non-zero elements, so `M_K = S^{-1}M` with
166	`S = A \ {0}`. Each generator of the finite `B`-module `M` is then killed by a
167	non-zero element of `A`; the product `f` of those annihilators (non-zero since
168	`A` is a domain) annihilates all of `M`, whence `M_f` is subsingleton and free. -/
169	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion
170	    (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
171	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
172	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
173	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
174	    (htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)) :
175	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
176	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
177	  -- The `A`/`B` scalar actions commute (A acts through `algebraMap A B`).
178	  have hcomm : ∀ (a : A) (b : B) (x : M), a • b • x = b • a • x := fun a b x => by
179	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a (b • x), ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a x,
180	      smul_smul, smul_smul, mul_comm]
181	  -- Torsion: every element of `M` is killed by a non-zero divisor of `A`.
182	  rw [LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff] at htors
183	  choose r hr using htors
184	  -- `r m ∈ A⁰` and `(r m) • m = 0`.
185	  -- A finite `B`-generating set of `M`.
186	  obtain ⟨S, hS⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top : (⊤ : Submodule B M).FG)
187	  -- The product of the chosen annihilators of the generators.
188	  set f : A := ∏ x ∈ S, r x with hf_def
189	  have hfmem : f ∈ nonZeroDivisors A := prod_mem fun x _ => (hr x).1
190	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hfmem
191	  -- `f` annihilates every generator, hence all of `span B S = ⊤ = M`.
192	  have hgen : ∀ x ∈ S, f • x = (0 : M) := by
193	    intro x hx
194	    classical
195	    rw [hf_def, ← Finset.prod_erase_mul S r hx, mul_smul, (hr x).2, smul_zero]
196	  have hfM : ∀ m : M, f • m = (0 : M) := by
197	    intro m
198	    have hmem : m ∈ Submodule.span B (↑S : Set M) := by rw [hS]; exact Submodule.mem_top
199	    induction hmem using Submodule.span_induction with
200	    | mem x hx => exact hgen x hx
201	    | zero => exact smul_zero f
202	    | add x y _ _ ihx ihy => rw [smul_add, ihx, ihy, add_zero]
203	    | smul b x _ ih => rw [hcomm, ih, smul_zero]
204	  -- `f • M = 0` ⟹ `M_f` subsingleton ⟹ free over `A_f`.
205	  refine ⟨f, hf0, ?_⟩
206	  haveI : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
207	    LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m => ⟨f, Submonoid.mem_powers f, hfM m⟩
208	  exact Module.Free.of_subsingleton _ _
209	
210	/-- **L3a — localisation of a short exact sequence is exact**
211	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact`, Nitsure §4). Given a short exact
212	sequence `0 → M' → M → M'' → 0` of `B`-modules and `f ∈ A`, restricting scalars
213	along `A → B` and localising every term at the powers of `f` yields a short
214	exact sequence `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules; the localised
215	`i_f` is injective and the localised `q_f` is surjective.
216	
217	Localisation of modules at a multiplicative set is exact, so it preserves the
218	injection `i`, the surjection `q`, and exactness at the middle term
219	(`LocalizedModule.map_injective` / `…map_surjective` / `…map_exact` applied to
220	the scalar-restricted maps). -/
221	theorem exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact
222	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A]
223	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
224	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
225	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
226	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
227	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
228	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
229	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
230	    (f : A) :
231	    Function.Injective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)) ∧
232	    Function.Surjective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) ∧
233	    Function.Exact (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A))
234	      (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) := by
235	  refine ⟨LocalizedModule.map_injective _ (i.restrictScalars A) hi,
236	    LocalizedModule.map_surjective _ (q.restrictScalars A) hq, ?_⟩
237	  exact LocalizedModule.map_exact (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)
238	    (q.restrictScalars A) hexact
239	
240	/-- **L3b — free transport across a finer localisation**
241	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport`, Nitsure §4). Let `N` be an
242	`A`-module and `f', f'' ∈ A` with `f = f' f''`. If `N_{f'}` is free over
243	`A_{f'}`, then `N_f` is free over `A_f`.
244	
245	Because `f = f' f''`, the localisation `A_f` is a localisation of `A_{f'}` at the
246	image of `f''` (`IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft` / `IsLocalization.Away.mul'`),
247	and `N_f` is the corresponding localisation of `N_{f'}`; a localisation of a free
248	module is free over the localised base ring (`Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`). -/
249	theorem free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul
250	    (A N : Type*) [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N]
251	    {f f' f'' : A} (hf : f = f' * f'')
252	    (hN' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f')
253	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N)) :
254	    Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
255	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
256	  subst hf
257	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away f')
258	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N) := hN'
259	  -- `A_f` also localises `f'' * f'` (= `f' * f''`).
260	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.Away (f'' * f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) := by
261	    rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance
262	  -- The ring map `A_{f'} → A_{f'f''}` and the `A`-scalar-tower it sits in.
263	  letI algA' : Algebra (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) :=
264	    (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft (R := A) (S := Localization.Away f')
265	      (P := Localization.Away (f' * f'')) f' f'').toAlgebra
266	  haveI htower : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) :=
267	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun a =>
268	      (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft_eq (S := Localization.Away f') f' f'' a).symm
269	  -- Restrict scalars along `A_{f'} → A_{f'f''}` to make `N_f` an `A_{f'}`-module.
270	  letI modA' : Module (Localization.Away f') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) :=
271	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')))
272	  haveI towerA'Af : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
273	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) :=
274	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
275	  haveI towerAA' : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f')
276	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) := by
277	    refine ⟨fun a a' x => ?_⟩
278	    have hcompA' : ∀ (b : Localization.Away f')
279	        (y : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N),
280	        b • y = algebraMap (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) b • y :=
281	      fun _ _ => rfl
282	    rw [hcompA' (a • a') x, hcompA' a' x, Algebra.smul_def, map_mul,
283	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) a,
284	      mul_smul, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
285	  -- `f'` acts invertibly on `N_f`: `f' * f''` does, and the commuting factor `f'` inherits it.
286	  have hunit : ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f',
287	      IsUnit (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N))
288	        (x : A)) := by
289	    have hprod : IsUnit (algebraMap A
290	        (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) (f' * f'')) :=
291	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units
292	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)
293	        ⟨f' * f'', Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
294	    rw [map_mul] at hprod
295	    have hcomm : Commute
296	        (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) f')
297	        (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) f'') :=
298	      (Commute.all _ _).map _
299	    have hf'unit := (hcomm.isUnit_mul_iff.mp hprod).1
300	    rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
301	    simpa [map_pow] using hf'unit.pow n
302	  -- The `A`-linear localisation map `N_{f'} → N_f`, upgraded to `A_{f'}`-linear.
303	  let φ : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N →ₗ[A]
304	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N :=
305	    IsLocalizedModule.lift (Submonoid.powers f') (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ N)
306	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ N) hunit
307	  let h : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N →ₗ[Localization.Away f']
308	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N :=
309	    LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers f') (Localization.Away f') φ
310	  -- `N_{f'}` is the base change of `N` to `A_{f'}`; the composite to `N_f` is the
311	  -- base change of `N` to `A_f`, so by cancellation `h` is the base change to `A_f`.
312	  have hbcA' : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away f')
313	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N) :=
314	    IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f') (Localization.Away f') _
315	  have hcomp : (LinearMap.restrictScalars A h) ∘ₗ
316	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N)
317	      = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N := by
318	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_extendScalarsOfIsLocalization]
319	    exact IsLocalizedModule.lift_comp _ _ _ hunit
320	  have hbcAf : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
321	      ((LinearMap.restrictScalars A h) ∘ₗ
322	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N)) := by
323	    rw [hcomp]
324	    exact IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (f' * f''))
325	      (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) _
326	  exact (hbcA'.of_comp hbcAf).free
327	
328	/-- **L3c — a short exact sequence with free ends has a free middle**
329	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split`, Nitsure §4). Let
330	`0 → P → Q → T → 0` be a short exact sequence of `R`-modules with both ends `P`
331	and `T` free over `R`. Then `Q` is free over `R`.
332	
333	The free quotient `T` is projective, so the surjection `Q → T` admits a section
334	(`Module.projective_lifting_property`); the splitting
335	(`Function.Exact.splitSurjectiveEquiv`) gives `Q ≃ₗ[R] P × T`, and `P × T` is
336	free, hence so is `Q` (`Module.Free.of_equiv`). -/
337	theorem free_of_shortExact_of_free_free
338	    (R P Q T : Type*) [CommRing R]
339	    [AddCommGroup P] [Module R P] [Module.Free R P]
340	    [AddCommGroup Q] [Module R Q]
341	    [AddCommGroup T] [Module R T] [Module.Free R T]
342	    (iota : P →ₗ[R] Q) (pi : Q →ₗ[R] T)
343	    (hiota : Function.Injective iota) (hpi : Function.Surjective pi)
344	    (hexact : Function.Exact iota pi) :
345	    Module.Free R Q := by
346	  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property pi LinearMap.id hpi
347	  obtain ⟨e, -⟩ := hexact.splitSurjectiveEquiv hiota ⟨l, hl⟩
348	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv e.symm
349	
350	/-- **L3 — splicing fact for a short exact sequence** (`lem:gf_splice_shortExact`,
351	Nitsure §4). Given a short exact sequence `0 → M' → M → M'' → 0` of `B`-modules
352	and non-zero `f', f'' ∈ A` such that `M'_{f'}` is free over `A_{f'}` and
353	`M''_{f''}` is free over `A_{f''}`, then for `f := f' f''` the localisation
354	`M_f` is free over `A_f`.
355	
356	Proof: localisation at the powers of `f` is exact, so it carries the SES to a
357	SES `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules. Both ends become free over
358	`A_f` (a free module over `A_{f'}` stays free under the further localisation
359	`A_{f'} → A_f`, via `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`), and a SES with free
360	(hence projective) quotient `M''_f` splits, so `M_f ≅ M'_f ⊕ M''_f` is free. -/
361	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact
362	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
363	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
364	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
365	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
366	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
367	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
368	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
369	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
370	    {f' f'' : A} (hf' : f' ≠ 0) (hf'' : f'' ≠ 0)
371	    (hM' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') M'))
372	    (hM'' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f'') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f'') M'')) :
373	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
374	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
375	  -- Take `f := f' * f''`, non-zero in the domain `A`.
376	  refine ⟨f' * f'', mul_ne_zero hf' hf'', ?_⟩
377	  -- (L3b) Both ends become free over `A_{f'f''}`.
378	  haveI hM'f : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
379	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M') :=
380	    free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul A M' (f := f' * f'') (f' := f') (f'' := f'') rfl hM'
381	  haveI hM''f : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
382	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M'') :=
383	    free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul A M'' (f := f' * f'') (f' := f'') (f'' := f')
384	      (mul_comm f' f'') hM''
385	  -- (L3a) The scalar-restricted SES stays exact after localising at the powers of `f'f''`.
386	  obtain ⟨hinj, hsurj, hex⟩ :=
387	    exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact A B M' M M'' i q hi hq hexact (f' * f'')
388	  -- (L3c) A short exact sequence of `A_{f'f''}`-modules with free ends has a free middle.
389	  exact free_of_shortExact_of_free_free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
390	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M')
391	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M)
392	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M'')
393	    (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) (i.restrictScalars A))
394	    (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) (q.restrictScalars A))
395	    hinj hsurj hex
396	
397	/-- **L4a — clearing one polynomial's denominators** (`lem:gf_clear_one_denominator`,
398	Nitsure §4 common-denominator step). For `A` a noetherian domain with fraction
399	field `K = Frac A` and a polynomial `p ∈ K[X_1,…,X_n]`, there is a non-zero
400	`g ∈ A` such that `p` lies in the image of the coefficient-extension map
401	`A_g[X_1,…,X_n] → K[X_1,…,X_n]`, where `A_g = Localization.Away g`; equivalently
402	every coefficient of `p` already lies in the subring `A_g ⊆ K`.
403	
404	The denominator-clearing engine: take `g` to be a common denominator of the
405	finitely many coefficients of `p` (Mathlib's
406	`IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples` over the support), so each coefficient
407	`c = a / g ∈ A_g`. The witness polynomial is the numerator polynomial scaled by
408	the unit `g⁻¹ ∈ A_g`. This is the shared primitive the Noether-normalisation L4
409	denominator-clear and the torsion-reindex (L5b) both consume. -/
410	theorem gf_clear_one_denominator
411	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
412	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (FractionRing A)) :
413	    ∃ (g : A) (hg : g ≠ 0),
414	      p ∈ Set.range (MvPolynomial.map
415	        (IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A)
416	          (show Submonoid.powers g ≤ Submonoid.comap (RingHom.id A) (nonZeroDivisors A) by
417	            rw [Submonoid.powers_le]
418	            simpa using mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg)
419	          : Localization.Away g →+* FractionRing A)) := by
420	  classical
421	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples (nonZeroDivisors A)
422	    p.support (fun i => MvPolynomial.coeff i p)
423	  refine ⟨s, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero s.2, ?_⟩
424	  have hle : Submonoid.powers (↑s : A) ≤ Submonoid.comap (RingHom.id A) (nonZeroDivisors A) := by
425	    rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact s.2
426	  set φ : Localization.Away (↑s : A) →+* FractionRing A :=
427	    IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A) hle with hφ
428	  have hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s : A)) ↑s) :=
429	    IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away (↑s : A))
430	      (⟨↑s, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩ : Submonoid.powers (↑s : A))
431	  have hnum : ∀ i, ∃ a : A, (algebraMap A (FractionRing A)) a =
432	      (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s) * MvPolynomial.coeff i p := by
433	    intro i
434	    by_cases hi : i ∈ p.support
435	    · obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := hs i hi
436	      exact ⟨a, by rw [ha, Algebra.smul_def]⟩
437	    · exact ⟨0, by rw [MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hi]; simp⟩
438	  choose a ha using hnum
439	  have hφs : φ (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)) ↑s)
440	      = algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s := by rw [hφ, IsLocalization.map_eq]; rfl
441	  have hee : (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
442	      * algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)) ↑s = 1 := by
443	    have h := Units.inv_mul hunit.unit
444	    rwa [hunit.unit_spec] at h
445	  have hφinv : φ (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
446	      * algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s = 1 := by
447	    rw [← hφs, ← map_mul, hee, map_one]
448	  -- The preimage polynomial: the numerator polynomial scaled by the unit `g⁻¹`.
449	  refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
450	        * (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)))
451	            (∑ i ∈ p.support, MvPolynomial.monomial i (a i))), ?_⟩
452	  apply MvPolynomial.ext
453	  intro i
454	  rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_map, MvPolynomial.coeff_C_mul, map_mul, MvPolynomial.coeff_map,
455	    IsLocalization.map_eq]
456	  have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff i (∑ j ∈ p.support, MvPolynomial.monomial j (a j)) = a i := by
457	    rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_sum]
458	    simp only [MvPolynomial.coeff_monomial, Finset.sum_ite_eq']
459	    by_cases hi : i ∈ p.support
460	    · rw [if_pos hi]
461	    · rw [if_neg hi]
462	      have hz : MvPolynomial.coeff i p = 0 := MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hi
463	      have h2 := ha i
464	      rw [hz, mul_zero] at h2
465	      exact (IsFractionRing.injective A (FractionRing A) (by rw [h2, map_zero])).symm
466	  rw [hcoeff, RingHom.id_apply, ha i, ← mul_assoc, hφinv, one_mul]
467	
468	/-- **L4 helper — a localisation lift into another injective localisation is injective.**
469	If `S` is a localisation of `R` at `M`, `g : R →+* P` sends `M` to units, and both
470	`algebraMap R S` and `g` are injective, then the induced `IsLocalization.lift hg : S →+* P`
471	is injective. (Used for the comparison maps `ν : B_g → B_K` and `ψ : A_g → K` in L4, both
472	localisations of a domain into a larger localisation.) -/
473	theorem isLocalization_lift_injective {R P : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing P]
474	    {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] {M : Submonoid R} [IsLocalization M S]
475	    {g : R →+* P} (hg : ∀ y : M, IsUnit (g (y : R)))
476	    (hSinj : Function.Injective (algebraMap R S))
477	    (hginj : Function.Injective g) :
478	    Function.Injective (IsLocalization.lift hg : S →+* P) := by
479	  rw [IsLocalization.lift_injective_iff]
480	  intro x y
481	  rw [hSinj.eq_iff, hginj.eq_iff]
482	
483	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
484	-- Deep nested localization instance synthesis (cf. `gf_torsion_reindex`) needs raised budgets.
485	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
486	-- The comparison-map (`ν`, `ψ`) + `aeval` assembly over stacked `Away` localizations is heavy.
487	/-- **L4 — Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators**
488	(`lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain
489	with fraction field `K`, and `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra that is a domain with
490	`B_K = K ⊗_A B ≠ 0`, there exist `g ∈ A`, `g ≠ 0`, a number `n`, and an
491	injective `A_g`-algebra map `φ : A_g[X_1,…,X_n] → B_g` (the `b_j := φ(X_j)`
492	algebraically independent over `K`) such that `B_g` is module-finite over
493	`A_g[X_1,…,X_n]` through `φ`.
494	
495	Here `A_g := Localization.Away g`, `B_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)`,
496	and module-finiteness is taken with respect to the `A_g[X]`-module structure on
497	`B_g` induced by `φ`.
498	
499	Proof (Nitsure §4): apply Noether normalisation `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`
500	over the field `K` to the finite-type `K`-algebra `B_K`, obtaining algebraically
501	independent `b̄_j ∈ B_K` with `B_K` module-finite over `K[b̄_1,…,b̄_n]`. Each
502	`b̄_j` is `1 ⊗ b_j` for some `b_j ∈ B`; choosing a finite generating set and
503	finitely many integral-dependence equations and letting `g` be a common
504	denominator of their coefficients makes `B_g` module-finite over
505	`A_g[b_1,…,b_n]`. -/
506	theorem exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial
507	    (A B : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
508	    [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
509	    (hBK : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B)) :
510	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
511	      (algBg : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
512	      (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)
513	            →ₐ[Localization.Away g] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
514	      Function.Injective φ ∧
515	      (letI := φ.toAlgebra;
516	        Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g))
517	          (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))) ∧
518	      -- The chosen `A_g`-algebra structure on `B_g` is compatible with the
519	      -- canonical `A → B → B_g` tower: `A_g → B_g` extends `A → B → B_g`. This
520	      -- pins `algBg` (otherwise an arbitrary instance) to the localisation map,
521	      -- which the cascade needs to bridge `LocalizedModule (powers g) B ≃ B_g`.
522	      (letI := algBg; ∀ a : A,
523	        algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))
524	            (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a)
525	          = algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)) (algebraMap A B a)) := by
526	  classical
527	  -- Step 1 (Nitsure §4): Noether normalisation over the fraction field
528	  -- `K = Frac A`, applied to the finite-type `K`-algebra `B_K = K ⊗_A B`. This
529	  -- yields algebraically independent generators (encoded by the injective AlgHom
530	  -- `gK`) over which `B_K` is module-finite.
531	  haveI : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := hBK
532	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType (FractionRing A) (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
533	    inferInstance
534	  obtain ⟨s, gK, hgK_inj, hgK_fin⟩ :=
535	    exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg (FractionRing A) (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B)
536	  -- `gK : MvPolynomial (Fin s) K →ₐ[K] B_K`, injective and module-finite.
537	  --
538	  -- ===================================================================
539	  -- Foundation (proved below, `g`-independent and reusable); there is no
540	  -- `sorry` here. The roadmap comment that follows documents the
541	  -- denominator-clearing assembly now realised below.
542	  -- ===================================================================
543	  --
544	  -- (F1) `A ↪ B`. Nontriviality of `B_K = K ⊗_A B` forces `algebraMap A B`
545	  -- injective: if `0 ≠ a ∈ A` had `algebraMap A B a = 0`, then `a` acts as `0`
546	  -- on every pure tensor (`a • (k ⊗ b) = k ⊗ (a • b) = k ⊗ 0 = 0`) yet `a` acts
547	  -- invertibly through the unit `algebraMap A K a`, forcing `B_K` subsingleton.
548	  have hAB : Function.Injective (algebraMap A B) := by
549	    rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
550	    intro a ha
551	    by_contra h0
552	    have hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) a) :=
553	      IsLocalization.map_units (FractionRing A)
554	        (⟨a, mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero h0⟩ : nonZeroDivisors A)
555	    have hsub : ∀ z : TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B, z = 0 := by
556	      intro z
557	      have haz : a • z = 0 := by
558	        induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
559	        | zero => simp
560	        | tmul k b =>
561	          rw [← TensorProduct.tmul_smul, Algebra.smul_def, ha, zero_mul, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
562	        | add x y hx hy => rw [smul_add, hx, hy, add_zero]
563	      have h2 : (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) a) • z = 0 := by
564	        rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]; exact haz
565	      exact (hunit.smul_eq_zero).mp h2
566	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
567	      ⟨fun x y => by rw [hsub x, hsub y]⟩
568	    exact (not_subsingleton _) ‹_›
569	  -- (F2) `B_K = K ⊗_A B` is the localisation of `B` at the image of the non-zero
570	  -- divisors of `A` (`IsLocalization.tensorRight`). This is the structural fact
571	  -- that lets us clear denominators of `gK (X j) ∈ B_K` down into `B` (and then
572	  -- into `B_g`), and that identifies the canonical maps `B → B_g → B_K`.
573	  letI : Algebra B (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
574	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid B (nonZeroDivisors A))
575	      (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
576	    IsLocalization.tensorRight (FractionRing A) (nonZeroDivisors A)
577	  -- (F3) A single common denominator `g0 ∈ A`, `g0 ≠ 0`, clearing all `s`
578	  -- generators `gK (X j)` into `B`: for each `j` there is `β_j ∈ B` with
579	  -- `algebraMap B B_K β_j = (algebraMap A B g0) • gK (X j)`.
580	  obtain ⟨den, hden⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples
581	    (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid B (nonZeroDivisors A)) (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin s))
582	    (fun j => gK (MvPolynomial.X j))
583	  obtain ⟨g0, hg0mem, hg0eq⟩ := den.2
584	  have hg0ne : g0 ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hg0mem
585	  -- (F4) `B_K` is integral over the polynomial subring `gK (K[X])` (module-finite
586	  -- ⟹ integral), so every element — in particular the image of each `A`-algebra
587	  -- generator of `B` — satisfies a monic equation with coefficients in `K[X]`.
588	  letI := gK.toAlgebra
589	  haveI hmf : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A))
590	      (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := hgK_fin
591	  haveI hint : Algebra.IsIntegral (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A))
592	      (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite _ _
593	  -- (F5) A finite `A`-algebra generating set `σ` of `B` (hence of `B_g` over
594	  -- `A_g`, and so of `B_g` over `A_g[X]` once `φ` is in hand).
595	  obtain ⟨σ, hσ⟩ : ∃ σ : Finset B, Algebra.adjoin A (↑σ : Set B) = ⊤ :=
596	    (inferInstance : Algebra.FiniteType A B).out
597	  -- (F6) the generators `gK (X j)` are algebraically independent over `K`
598	  -- (`hgK_inj`, since `gK = aeval (gK ∘ X)`). Restricting scalars along the
599	  -- injection `A_g ↪ K` will descend this to independence over `A_g`, which is
600	  -- exactly injectivity of `φ` (see the INJECTIVITY step of the roadmap).
601	  have hAI : AlgebraicIndependent (FractionRing A) (fun j => gK (MvPolynomial.X j)) := by
602	    rw [algebraicIndependent_iff_injective_aeval]
603	    have hgKaeval : (MvPolynomial.aeval (fun j => gK (MvPolynomial.X j)) :
604	        MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A) →ₐ[FractionRing A] _) = gK := by
605	      apply MvPolynomial.algHom_ext; intro j; simp
606	    rw [hgKaeval]; exact hgK_inj

608	  -- Step 2 (denominator clearing → `g1`).  `B_K` is the localisation of the
609	  -- polynomial ring `MvPoly A` at the constants `C(A⁰)`, with localised ring
610	  -- `MvPoly K` (`MvPolynomial.isLocalization`).  Each `A`-algebra generator `x ∈ σ`
611	  -- maps to an element of `B_K` integral over `MvPoly K` (Step 1 finiteness `hint`);
612	  -- the collapsing lemma `IsIntegral.exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization`
613	  -- multiplies it by a constant `C a` (`a ∈ A⁰`) to make it integral over `MvPoly A`.

615	  letI algPAK : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin s) A) (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A)) :=
616	    MvPolynomial.algebraMvPolynomial
617	  haveI islocPAK : IsLocalization
618	      (Submonoid.map (MvPolynomial.C : A →+* MvPolynomial (Fin s) A) (nonZeroDivisors A))

620	    MvPolynomial.isLocalization (nonZeroDivisors A) (FractionRing A)

626	      (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (FractionRing A)) (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) :=
627	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl

631	          algebraMap B (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) x) := by
632	    intro x

639	    refine ⟨a, ha_mem, ?_⟩

641	        algebraMap B (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) B) x
642	        = (m : MvPolynomial (Fin s) A) •

965	/-- **L5a — the generic-rank short exact sequence** (`lem:gf_generic_rank_ses`,
966	Nitsure §4 inductive step). For `A` a noetherian domain and `d ≥ 0`, a finite
967	module `N` over the polynomial ring `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `m ∈ ℕ`
968	and an injective `P_d`-linear map `φ : P_d^{⊕m} → N` whose cokernel
969	`T := N ⧸ range φ` is a torsion `P_d`-module. Equivalently, a short exact
970	sequence `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N → T → 0` with `T` torsion.
971	
972	`m` is the generic rank: `dim_{Frac P_d} (Frac P_d ⊗_{P_d} N)`. The map `φ`
973	is the `P_d`-linear combination of `m` lifts `v : Fin m → N` whose images form a
974	`Frac P_d`-basis of the localisation `N_Q := LocalizedModule (P_d)⁰ N`. This step
975	is built over `P_d` directly — no inversion of any `g ∈ A` is required. -/
976	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
977	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
978	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
979	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
980	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
981	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
982	        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
983	      Function.Injective φ ∧
984	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) := by
985	  classical
986	  -- Notation: `P = A[X_1,…,X_d]`, its non-zero divisors `S`, fraction field `K`,
987	  -- localisation `NK = N_Q`, and the localisation map `ℓ : N → NK`.
988	  let P := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A
989	  let S := nonZeroDivisors P
990	  let K := Localization S
991	  let NK := LocalizedModule S N
992	  let ℓ : N →ₗ[P] NK := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N
993	  -- `NK` is a finite-dimensional `K`-vector space; take a basis indexed by `Fin m`.
994	  let m := Module.finrank K NK
995	  let b : Module.Basis (Fin m) K NK := Module.finBasis K NK
996	  -- Lift each basis vector along `ℓ`, clearing its denominator.
997	  obtain ⟨lift, hlift⟩ :
998	      ∃ lift : Fin m → N × S, ∀ i, ((lift i).2 : P) • b i = ℓ (lift i).1 := by
999	    refine ⟨fun i => (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose, fun i => ?_⟩
1000	    exact (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose_spec
1001	  let v : Fin m → N := fun i => (lift i).1
1002	  -- The denominators map to units of `K`.
1003	  let u : Fin m → Kˣ := fun i => (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit
1004	  have hlv : ∀ i, ℓ (v i) = (↑(u i) : K) • b i := by
1005	    intro i
1006	    have hu : (↑(u i) : K) = algebraMap P K ((lift i).2 : P) :=
1007	      (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit_spec
1008	    rw [hu, algebraMap_smul]
1009	    exact (hlift i).symm
1010	  -- The images `ℓ (v i)` are linearly independent over `K` (unit multiples of a basis).
1011	  have hLIK : LinearIndependent K (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
1012	    have h := b.linearIndependent.units_smul u
1013	    have heq : (fun i => ℓ (v i)) = (u • (⇑b)) := by
1014	      funext i; rw [hlv i]; rfl
1015	    rw [heq]; exact h
1016	  -- Restrict scalars to `P` (the inclusion `P → K` is injective), then descend to `v`.
1017	  have hLIP : LinearIndependent P (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
1018	    refine hLIK.restrict_scalars ?_
1019	    intro x y hxy
1020	    apply IsFractionRing.injective P K
1021	    rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
1022	    exact hxy
1023	  have hLIv : LinearIndependent P v := LinearIndependent.of_comp ℓ hLIP
1024	  -- The map `φ : P^{⊕m} → N`, `e_i ↦ v i`.
1025	  refine ⟨m, Fintype.linearCombination P v, ?_, ?_⟩
1026	  · -- Injectivity of `φ` is exactly linear independence of `v`.
1027	    rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot']
1028	    intro g hg
1029	    rw [Fintype.linearCombination_apply] at hg
1030	    funext i
1031	    exact Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hLIv g hg i
1032	  · -- The cokernel `T = N ⧸ range φ` is torsion: every element is killed by a
1033	    -- non-zero divisor of `P`.
1034	    intro x
1035	    obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _ x
1036	    -- The images `ℓ (v i)` span `NK` over `K` (they are unit multiples of a basis).
1037	    have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range (fun i => ℓ (v i))) = ⊤ := by
1038	      refine top_unique ?_
1039	      rw [← b.span_eq, Submodule.span_le]
1040	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
1041	      have hbi : b i = (↑(u i) : K)⁻¹ • ℓ (v i) := by
1042	        rw [hlv i, smul_smul, inv_mul_cancel₀ (u i).ne_zero, one_smul]
1043	      rw [hbi]
1044	      exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
1045	    -- Hence `ℓ n` is a `K`-linear combination of the `ℓ (v i)`.
1046	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := (Submodule.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun K).mp
1047	      (show ℓ n ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range fun i => ℓ (v i)) by
1048	        rw [hspan]; exact Submodule.mem_top)
1049	    -- Clear the denominators of the finitely many coefficients `c i`.
1050	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples S Finset.univ c
1051	    have ha : ∀ i, ∃ y : P, algebraMap P K y = (s : P) • c i := fun i =>
1052	      hs i (Finset.mem_univ i)
1053	    choose a hae using ha
1054	    -- After multiplying by `s`, `ℓ (s • n)` becomes `ℓ (φ a)`.
1055	    have key : ℓ ((s : P) • n) = ℓ (Fintype.linearCombination P v a) := by
1056	      rw [map_smul, ← hc, Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]
1057	      refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1058	      rw [map_smul]
1059	      -- `(s:P) • (c i • ℓ v_i) = a i • ℓ v_i`
1060	      rw [← algebraMap_smul K ((s : P)) (c i • ℓ (v i)), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def,
1061	        ← hae i, algebraMap_smul]
1062	    -- So `s • n - φ a` lies in the kernel of `ℓ`, hence is killed by some `t ∈ S`.
1063	    have hker : ℓ ((s : P) • n - Fintype.linearCombination P v a) = 0 := by
1064	      rw [map_sub, key, sub_self]
1065	    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S ℓ).mp hker
1066	    -- The non-zero divisor `t * s` annihilates the class of `n` in `T`.
1067	    refine ⟨t * s, ?_⟩
1068	    rw [smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at ht
1069	    have ht2 : (↑t : P) • ((↑s : P) • n) = (↑t : P) • Fintype.linearCombination P v a := ht
1070	    have hmem : (↑(t * s) : P) • n ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination P v) := by
1071	      rw [Submonoid.coe_mul, mul_smul, ht2, ← map_smul]
1072	      exact LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _
1073	    -- Conclude `(t * s) • ⟦n⟧ = 0`.
1074	    rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]
1075	    exact hmem

1077	/-- **L5b.1 — annihilator extraction for the torsion module**
1078	(`lem:gf_torsion_annihilator`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`,
1079	and `T` a finite *torsion* module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exists a
1080	non-zero `F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`. Since `P_d` is a domain, a non-zero-divisor
1081	annihilator (provided by `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`) is the
1082	same thing as a non-zero one. -/
1083	theorem gf_torsion_annihilator
1084	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
1085	    (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1086	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1087	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
1088	    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
1089	      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by
1090	  obtain ⟨F, hFann, hFnzd⟩ := Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors htors
1091	  refine ⟨F, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hFnzd, ?_⟩
1092	  rw [← Submodule.annihilator_top]
1093	  exact hFann
1094	
1095	/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)
1096	
1097	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
1098	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
1099	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
1100	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
1101	`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
1102	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
1103	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
1104	domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
1105	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
1106	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/
1107	
1108	section NagataNormalization
1109	
1110	-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
1111	-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
1112	open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List
1113	
1114	/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
1115	private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
1116	    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
1117	    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
1118	      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
1119	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1120	  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
1121	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1122	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1123	    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
1124	    congr 1
1125	    cases i using Fin.cases with
1126	    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
1127	    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]
1128	
1129	variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
1130	variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)
1131	
1132	local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
1133	local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1
1134	
1135	omit [IsDomain k] in
1136	variable {f v} in
1137	private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind
1138	
1139	/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
1140	`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
1141	private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
1142	    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
1143	  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i
1144	
1145	omit [IsDomain k] in
1146	private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
1147	  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp
1148	
1149	/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
1150	private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
1151	  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))
1152	
1153	omit [IsDomain k] in
1154	private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1155	    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
1156	  intro h
1157	  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
1158	  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
1159	    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
1160	  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
1161	    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h
1162	
1163	private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
1164	    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
1165	  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
1166	  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
1167	    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,
1168	    ite_false, one_smul, map_add, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, finSuccEquiv_X_succ, algebraMap_eq]
1169	  have h (i : Fin n) :
1170	      (Polynomial.C (X (R := k) i) + Polynomial.X ^ r i.succ) ^ v i.succ ≠ 0 :=
1171	    pow_ne_zero (v i.succ) (leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp <| by simp [add_comm, leadingCoeff_X_pow_add_C])
1172	  rw [natDegree_mul (by simp [ha]) (mul_ne_zero (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
1173	    (fun i _ ↦ h i))), natDegree_mul (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun i _ ↦ h i)),
1174	    natDegree_prod _ _ (fun i _ ↦ h i), natDegree_finSuccEquiv, degreeOf_C]
1175	  simpa only [natDegree_pow, zero_add, natDegree_X, mul_one, Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_mul,
1176	    add_right_inj] using Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ ↦ by
1177	    rw [add_comm (Polynomial.C _), natDegree_X_pow_add_C, mul_comm])
1178	
1179	private lemma degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne (hv : v ∈ f.support) (hw : w ∈ f.support) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1180	    (T f <| monomial v <| coeff v f).degreeOf 0 ≠
1181	    (T f <| monomial w <| coeff w f).degreeOf 0 := by
1182	  rw [degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hv, degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hw]
1183	  refine sum_r_mul_ne f v w (fun i ↦ ?_) (fun i ↦ ?_) ne <;>
1184	  exact lt_of_le_of_lt ((monomial_le_degreeOf i ‹_›).trans (degreeOf_le_totalDegree f i)) (by lia)
1185	
1186	private lemma leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t :
1187	    (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) ((monomial v) (coeff v f)))).leadingCoeff =
1188	    algebraMap k _ (coeff v f) := by
1189	  rw [monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_fintype]
1190	  · simp only [map_mul, map_prod, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_prod]
1191	    rw [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, algHom_C, algebraMap_eq, finSuccEquiv_apply, eval₂Hom_C, coe_comp]
1192	    simp only [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, Function.comp_apply, leadingCoeff_C, map_pow,
1193	      leadingCoeff_pow, algebraMap_eq]
1194	    have : ∀ j, ((finSuccEquiv k n) ((T1 f) 1 (X j))).leadingCoeff = 1 := fun j ↦ by
1195	      by_cases h : j = 0
1196	      · simp [h, finSuccEquiv_apply]
1197	      · simp only [aeval_eq_bind₁, bind₁_X_right, if_neg h, one_smul, map_add, map_pow]
1198	        obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := Fin.exists_succ_eq.mpr h
1199	        simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, add_comm]
1200	    simp only [this, one_pow, Finset.prod_const_one, mul_one]
1201	  exact fun i ↦ pow_zero _
1202	
1203	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
1204	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
1205	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
1206	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
1207	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1208	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1209	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1210	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1211	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1212	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1213	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1214	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1215	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1216	    intro x hx
1217	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1218	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1219	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1220	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2
1221	  have coeff : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v + ∑ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, h x))).leadingCoeff =
1222	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1223	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1224	    rw [add_comm]
1225	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1226	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1227	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1228	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq
1229	    exact (Finset.sup_lt_iff <| Ne.bot_lt (fun x ↦ h2 <| degree_eq_bot.mp x)).mpr vs
1230	  refine ⟨v, vin, ?_⟩
1231	  rw [leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t] at coeff
1232	  nth_rw 2 [← f.support_sum_monomial_coeff]
1233	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton_of_mem vin h, coeff, algebraMap_eq]
1234	
1235	/-- **L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables: monic in the distinguished variable**
1236	(`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a domain and `0 ≠ F ∈
1237	A[X_0,…,X_m]`, there is a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a non-zero
1238	`g ∈ A` such that, after inverting `g`, the image of `e F` in `A_g[X_0,…,X_m]` —
1239	viewed via `finSuccEquiv` as a univariate polynomial in `X_0` — has a *unit* leading
1240	coefficient. (Variable convention: `finSuccEquiv` singles out `X_0`, not `X_m`; the
1241	choice is immaterial up to renaming.) -/
1242	theorem gf_nagata_monic_lastVar
1243	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (m : ℕ)
1244	    (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
1245	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1246	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),
1247	      IsUnit
1248	        (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) m
1249	          (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff := by
1250	  obtain ⟨v, vin, hlc⟩ := T_leadingcoeff_eq F hF
1251	  have hg0 : coeff v F ≠ 0 := mem_support_iff.mp vin
1252	  refine ⟨coeff v F, hg0, T F, ?_⟩
1253	  set g := coeff v F with hg
1254	  haveI : Nontrivial (Localization.Away g) := by
1255	    have hle : Submonoid.powers g ≤ nonZeroDivisors A := by
1256	      rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0
1257	    exact (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away g) hle).nontrivial
1258	  have hgunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g) :=
1259	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g
1260	  have hCunit : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g)
1261	      : MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) := hgunit.map MvPolynomial.C
1262	  have hne : (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)))
1263	      (finSuccEquiv A m (T F F)).leadingCoeff ≠ 0 := by
1264	    rw [hlc, MvPolynomial.map_C]; exact hCunit.ne_zero
1265	  rw [finSuccEquiv_map_comm, leadingCoeff_map_of_leadingCoeff_ne_zero _ hne, hlc,
1266	    MvPolynomial.map_C]
1267	  exact hCunit
1268	
1269	end NagataNormalization
1270	
1271	/-- Compatibility of `finSuccEquiv` with the constant-variable inclusion
1272	`rename Fin.succ`: the composite `S = R[X_1,…,X_n] → R[X_0,…,X_n] → S[X_0]` is the
1273	coefficient inclusion `Polynomial.C`. Project-local because Mathlib only records the
1274	`optionEquivLeft` variant (`finSuccEquiv_rename_finSuccEquiv`). -/
1275	private theorem finSuccEquiv_rename_succ
1276	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :
1277	    MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s) = Polynomial.C s := by
1278	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1279	  | C r => rw [MvPolynomial.rename_C, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]; rfl
1280	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1281	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1282	    rw [map_mul, map_mul, hp, MvPolynomial.rename_X, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_X_succ,
1283	      Polynomial.C_mul]
1284	
1285	/-- **L5b.3 — single-variable elimination engine** (shared)
1286	(`lem:gf_mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_monic`, Nitsure §4). Let `R` be a commutative
1287	ring and `p ∈ R[X_0,…,X_n]` a polynomial whose image under `finSuccEquiv` (a
1288	univariate polynomial in `X_0` over `S := R[X_1,…,X_n]`) has a *unit* leading
1289	coefficient — i.e. `p` is monic in `X_0` up to a unit. Then `R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p)` is
1290	module-finite over `S = R[X_1,…,X_n]`, the structure being via the constant
1291	inclusion `S ↪ R[X_0,…,X_n]` (`rename Fin.succ`) followed by `Ideal.Quotient.mk`.
1292	
1293	Encoded as `RingHom.Finite` of that composite ring map (which unfolds to
1294	`Module.Finite S (R[X_0,…,X_n]/(p))` with the exotic algebra structure) to avoid an
1295	in-statement `letI` whose `Module` synthesis loops. The proof rescales `finSuccEquiv p`
1296	by the inverse of its unit leading coefficient to a monic polynomial generating the
1297	same ideal, invokes `Polynomial.Monic.finite_quotient`, and transports along the
1298	`S`-algebra isomorphism induced by `finSuccEquiv`. -/
1299	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
1300	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
1301	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
1302	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
1303	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1304	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
1305	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
1306	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
1307	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1308	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
1309	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
1310	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
1311	  have hcP :
1312	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
1313	    hc.map Polynomial.C
1314	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1315	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
1316	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
1317	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
1318	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
1319	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1320	      = Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p} :=
1321	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)
1322	  have hfinstd : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1323	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) := by
1324	    rw [← hspan]; exact hmonic.finite_quotient
1325	  have hJ : Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1326	      = Ideal.map ((MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv :
1327	          MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R →+* Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R))
1328	        (Ideal.span {p}) := by
1329	    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
1330	  let ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1331	      ≃+* (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
1332	    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {p}) (Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1333	      (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n).toRingEquiv hJ
1334	  have hsmul : ∀ s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R,
1335	      ψ (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1336	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) s)
1337	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1338	          (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1339	          s := by
1340	    intro s
1341	    change ψ (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s))
1342	      = Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Polynomial.C s)
1343	    rw [Ideal.quotientEquiv_mk]
1344	    exact congrArg _ (finSuccEquiv_rename_succ R n s)
1345	  let Ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1346	      ≃ₐ[MvPolynomial (Fin n) R]
1347	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=
1348	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv hsmul
1349	  exact @Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1350	    (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})
1351	    (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1352	    _ _ _ _ algI.toModule hfinstd Ψ.symm.toLinearEquiv
1353	
1354	/-- Pull back an `R`-module structure along an additive equivalence `e : M ≃+ N`, defining
1355	`r • y := e (r • e.symm y)`. With this structure `e` is `R`-linear; used in the torsion
1356	reindex to transport the reindexed module structure from the `P`-localisation of the torsion
1357	module to the goal's `A`-localisation. -/
1358	@[reducible] def pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1359	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] (e : M ≃+ N) : Module R N where
1360	  smul r y := e (r • e.symm y)
1361	  one_smul y := by change e (1 • e.symm y) = y; rw [one_smul, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1362	  mul_smul r s y := by
1363	    change e ((r * s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm (e (s • e.symm y)))
1364	    rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, mul_smul]
1365	  smul_zero r := by change e (r • e.symm 0) = 0; rw [map_zero, smul_zero, map_zero]
1366	  smul_add r x y := by
1367	    change e (r • e.symm (x + y)) = e (r • e.symm x) + e (r • e.symm y)
1368	    rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
1369	  add_smul r s y := by
1370	    change e ((r + s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm y) + e (s • e.symm y)
1371	    rw [add_smul, map_add]
1372	  zero_smul y := by change e (0 • e.symm y) = 0; rw [zero_smul, map_zero]
1373	
1374	/-- Module-finiteness transports across the pulled-back structure of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1375	if `M` is a finite `R`-module then so is `N` under the pulled-back action. -/
1376	theorem finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1377	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] (e : M ≃+ N) :
1378	    @Module.Finite R N _ _ (pullbackModuleAddEquiv e) := by

840	  -- proof entirely from term-mode whiskering equalities and chain them with `.trans`.
841	  have h1 := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α c
842	  -- the two `homEquiv_unit` expansions, with all implicits fixed by the stated types.
843	  have huA : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
844	      = adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c ≫ f) :=
845	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₂ c d (α.app c ≫ f)
846	  have huB : adj₁.homEquiv c d f = adj₁.unit.app c ≫ R₁.map f :=
847	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₁ c d f
848	  -- LHS transpose, in left-bracketed shape.
849	  have e1 : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
850	      = (adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c)) ≫ R₂.map f :=
851	    huA.trans <| (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₂.unit.app c) (R₂.map_comp (α.app c) f)).trans
852	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
853	  -- RHS transpose, in the same left-bracketed shape.
854	  have e2 : adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d
855	      = (adj₁.unit.app c ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app (L₁.obj c))
856	          ≫ R₂.map f :=
857	    (CategoryTheory.eq_whisker huB
858	        ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d)).trans <|
859	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
860	        (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₁.unit.app c)
861	          ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).naturality f)).trans
862	          (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
863	  exact e1.trans ((CategoryTheory.eq_whisker h1.symm (R₂.map f)).trans e2.symm)

865	/-- **Unit coherence (`map_comp` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`).** The composite
866	analogue of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`: the Mathlib free-pullback comparison at a composite
867	morphism `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`. Project-local.
868	
869	Transposing both sides under the composite pullback-pushforward adjunction: the LHS collapses
870	by `homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app` to `uTPU (b ≫ a) ≫ (conjugate of pullbackComp.hom)`, the
871	conjugate is `(pushforwardComp).inv` via `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` + `conjugateEquiv_comm`,
872	and the RHS collapses by `homEquiv` naturality to `uTPU a ≫ pushforward a (uTPU b)`;
873	both reduce to the unit-section identity (`pushforwardComp_inv_app_app = 𝟙`). -/
874	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
875	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
876	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))
877	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
878	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
879	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) := by
880	  -- Work with the Scheme-level adjunctions so `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` lines up.
881	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
882	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
883	  -- abbreviations
884	  set adjA := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a
885	  set adjB := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b
886	  set adjBA := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)
887	  -- LHS: collapse via the mate-compatibility helper (term-mode `.trans`, so `pullbackComp`
888	  -- stays OPAQUE and matching is up to defeq rather than syntactic `rw`).
889	  have hL := homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
890	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom
891	      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)))
892	  -- transpose of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` (used via defeq).
893	  have hL2 : adjBA.homEquiv _ _
894	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)))
895	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) :=
896	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
897	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))
898	  have huA : adjA.homEquiv _ _
899	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a))
900	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) :=
901	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
902	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)
903	  have huB : adjB.homEquiv _ _
904	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b))
905	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) :=
906	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
907	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)
908	  -- the conjugate of `pullbackComp.hom` is `(pushforwardComp).inv`.
909	  have hcomm := CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_comm (adj₁ := adjA.comp adjB) (adj₂ := adjBA)
910	    (show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv = 𝟙 _
911	      from (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom_inv_id)
912	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at hcomm
913	  have hConj : CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
914	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom
915	      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).inv :=
916	    (CategoryTheory.Iso.hom_comp_eq_id _).mp hcomm
917	  -- RHS computation, term-mode (so the Scheme/SheafOfModules `pullback` defeq is bridged).
918	  have hR : (adjA.comp adjB).homEquiv _ _
919	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
920	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
921	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b))
922	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ≫
923	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
924	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) := by
925	    rw [Adjunction.comp_homEquiv]
926	    change adjA.homEquiv _ _ (adjB.homEquiv _ _ (_ ≫ _)) = _
927	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, huB, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, huA]
928	    rfl
929	  -- the section-level identity: `(pushforwardComp).inv.app` is the identity on sections.
930	  have hMid : SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
931	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)
932	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ≫
933	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
934	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) := by
935	    -- definitional: `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` sections are `φ.hom.app`,
936	    -- `pushforwardComp_inv_app_app = 𝟙`, and `(b ≫ a)⁻¹ U = b⁻¹(a⁻¹ U)`.
937	    rfl
938	  -- assemble in steps to avoid a single large `isDefEq` over the opaque `pullbackComp`.
939	  have e1 := hL.trans (congrArg
940	    (· ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
941	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom).app (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)) hL2)
942	  have e2 := congrArg
943	    (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
944	      NatTrans.app · (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)) hConj
945	  exact e1.trans (e2.trans (hMid.trans hR.symm))

947	/-- **Free coherence (`map_comp`).** Composite analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_id`: the
948	free-pullback isomorphism at a composite `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition
949	`pullbackComp`. Reduces, by coproduct extensionality (`free = ∐ unit`), to the unit coherence
950	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Project-local. -/
951	lemma pullbackFreeIso_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) (I : Type u) :
952	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
953	        (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
954	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom ≫
955	        (pullbackFreeIso b I).hom := by
956	  haveI := opensMap_final (b ≫ a)
957	  haveI := opensMap_final a
958	  haveI := opensMap_final b
959	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit
960	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
961	      SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) _ _
962	    (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I)) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
963	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj]
964	  -- Pure term-mode (positional `rw`/`simp` fail under the `SheafOfModules`/`X.Modules` diamond).
965	  -- Both injections reduce, via `pullbackComp.hom` naturality and the free-cofan comparison
966	  -- `pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom`, to `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` whiskered.
967	  -- the free-cofan comparison, restated in `pullbackFreeIso` form (defeq) so `congrArg` matches.
968	  -- each pullback changes the base ring sheaf: `Tx ↝ Ty ↝ Tz`.
969	  have key_ba : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (b ≫ a)).map
970	          (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
971	        (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
972	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
973	        SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i :=
974	    SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
975	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) (I := I) i
976	  have key_a : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
977	        (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom
978	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ≫
979	        SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Ty.ringCatSheaf) i :=
980	    SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
981	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) (I := I) i
982	  have key_b : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Ty.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
983	        (pullbackFreeIso b I).hom
984	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) ≫
985	        SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i :=
986	    SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom
987	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) (I := I) i
988	  -- LHS: naturality of `pullbackComp.hom` + free-cofan comparison at `b ≫ a`.
989	  have hLHS :
990	      (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
991	          SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
992	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
993	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
994	          (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
995	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
996	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))) ≫
997	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i) :=
998	    calc (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
999	            SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
1000	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
1001	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I) ≫
1002	            (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom
1003	        = ((SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
1004	              SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
1005	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
1006	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app
1007	              (SheafOfModules.free (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I)) ≫
1008	            (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom := (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
1009	      _ = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
1010	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback (b ≫ a)).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i)) ≫
1011	            (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom :=
1012	          congrArg (· ≫ (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom)
1013	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.naturality
1014	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i))
1015	      _ = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
1016	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback (b ≫ a)).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
1017	              (pullbackFreeIso (b ≫ a) I).hom := Category.assoc _ _ _

1019	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
1020	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i) :=
1021	          congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app
1022	            (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫ ·) key_ba
1023	      _ = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
1024	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))) ≫
1025	              (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tz.ringCatSheaf) i) := (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm

1028	      (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙
1029	          SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
1030	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
1031	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom
1032	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
1033	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
1034	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Ty.ringCatSheaf) i) :=
1035	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_comp
1036	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i))
1037	        (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom).symm.trans
1038	      ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map key_a).trans
1039	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_comp
1040	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a))

1046	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom ≫ (pullbackFreeIso b I).hom

1048	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
1049	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) ≫

1052	            SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map
1053	            (SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) i) ≫
1054	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (pullbackFreeIso a I).hom ≫ (pullbackFreeIso b I).hom

1056	              SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)).map

1083	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
1084	
1085	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
1086	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
1087	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
1088	
1089	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
1090	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
1091	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
1092	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
1093	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
1094	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
1095	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
1096	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
1097	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
1098	  map_id X := by
1099	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
1100	    ext z
1101	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1102	    | _ x =>
1103	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
1104	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
1105	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
1106	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
1107	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
1108	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
1109	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
1110	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
1111	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1112	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1113	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
1114	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
1115	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
1116	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
1117	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
1118	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
1119	    ext z
1120	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1121	    | _ x =>
1122	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
1123	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
1124	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
1125	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
1126	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
